drafts.title: Why your Co-Star reading feels generic
drafts.hook: Why does every Leo get the same horoscope? Because Western astrology uses 1 data point (sun sign). Korean Saju uses 8. Here's why that matters in 2026 🧵
body: Western astrology: Sun sign + maybe rising/moon = 3 variables. ~12 archetypes. 8 billion humans squeezed into 12 boxes.
Korean Saju (사주): 4 pillars × 2 characters each = 8 characters. Each character is one of 22 possible. Permutations: 518,400 distinct base charts. Plus timing cycles.
That's why Saju readings feel surgical. It isn't a horoscope. It's a personality + timing engine built over 5,000 years in East Asia.

And it gets weirder: each pillar maps to a life domain.
• Year = ancestry/society
• Month = career
• Day = self + romance
• Hour = hidden self + late life
If you've ever wondered why your Korean friend asks for your birth TIME (not just date), this is why. The hour pillar changes 12 times a day. Without it, you're missing 25% of the chart.
Free English Saju (no signup): → https://cheonmyeongdang.com/en/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d1

drafts.title: 12 zodiac signs vs 60-year cycle
drafts.hook: Western astrology repeats every 1 year. Korean Saju repeats every 60. That's not a flex — it's a math claim. Here's what 60-year cycles predict that 1-year cycles can't 🧵
body: The Korean (and Chinese) sexagenary cycle pairs 10 Heavenly Stems × 12 Earthly Branches = 60 unique year-codes. 2026 is 동오 (Bing-Wu, Red Horse).
Last Red Horse year: 1966. Before that: 1906, 1846, 1786. The pattern? Cultural inflection points. Civil rights, post-war booms, AI revolutions.
Western horoscopes give you a vibe for the week. Saju gives you the macro tide your decade is riding.
This is why Korean families historically used Saju for marriage timing, business launch dates, and even baby planning. The math has 60 years of texture, not 12.
Try yours free in English. See your decade-tide (대운) projected to 2034 → https://cheonmyeongdang.com/en/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d2

drafts.title: Mercury retrograde vs 삼한살
drafts.hook: Western astrology has Mercury retrograde (3x a year). Korean Saju has 삼한살 (Samhansal) — a 3-year unlucky cycle hitting only specific zodiac groups. Way more surgical 🧵
body: Samhansal divides the 12 Earthly Branches into 4 groups of 3. Each group has a 3-year window where they're statistically more accident-prone, decision-impaired, financially leaky.
Born in Tiger/Horse/Dog years? Your Samhansal is Snake/Horse/Sheep years. So 2026 (Horse) is mid-Samhansal for you.
Born in Rat/Dragon/Monkey? Yours is Tiger/Rabbit/Dragon (2022–2024 — just exited).
This isn't superstition theatre — Korean insurance actuaries and traditional doctors still glance at this when assessing risk windows.
Check your own Samhansal status (free, English) → https://cheonmyeongdang.com/en/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d3

drafts.title: Birth chart depth: 1 vs 8
drafts.hook: I asked Co-Star and a Korean Saju site the same question: 'Why do I keep dating the wrong people?' One gave me a 4-line meme. The other gave me my Day Master's element conflict. Guess which helped 🧵
body: Day Master = the Heavenly Stem of your Day Pillar. It's YOU. Your Day Master is one of 10 elements: Yang Wood, Yin Wood, Yang Fire, Yin Fire, etc.
Romantic compatibility in Saju = your Day Master's element relationship with theirs. Wood feeds Fire (good). Fire melts Metal (tense). Water extinguishes Fire (toxic).
If you're Yang Fire and you keep dating Yin Water? Saju would have flagged that on date 1.

Western: 'You're a Sagittarius, they're a Pisces, mixed.' 
Saju: 'Your Day Master is 정화 (Yin Fire). Their 일간 is 임수 (Yang Water). Direct extinguishment. Romance will mirror water-on-fire dynamics.'

drafts.title: Why Saju isn't 'Chinese astrology'
drafts.hook: Friendly correction — Korean Saju and Chinese BaZi share the same character system but diverged 800 years ago. Here's what's specifically Korean about Saju, and why it matters 🧵
body: Both use 60-year sexagenary cycles + 8 characters. That's the shared core.

Korean Saju layered on:
• 십신 (Ten Gods) emphasis on social/family roles
• 대운 (10-year luck cycles) read for women too (Chinese tradition often skipped this)
• 신살 (Spirit Killers) — 22 named patterns unique to Korean lineage
Chinese BaZi tends toward business/wealth. Korean Saju tends toward relationships, education, and family timing — reflecting Confucian Korea's social structure.
If you've tried 'BaZi calculators' and felt the analysis was cold, try the Korean version. It reads people, not just markets.
Free Korean-method Saju in English → https://cheonmyeongdang.com/en/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d5

drafts.title: Four Pillars 101: Year
drafts.hook: Saju 101 — Pillar #1 of 4: the YEAR PILLAR. This is your ancestral software. Not your destiny — your inherited operating system. Here's how to read it 🧵
body: The Year Pillar = the year you were born, encoded in 1 Heavenly Stem + 1 Earthly Branch. 2026 = 동오 (Bing Wu, Yang Fire + Horse).

What it tells you:
• Generational karma (what your family/society handed you)
• Childhood environment
• Public reputation seed
Example: Born 1995 = 을해 (Yi Hai, Yin Wood + Pig). Carries themes of late-bloomer creativity, water-fed wood = research/healing tendencies.
Common mistake: thinking 'I'm a Horse year' = you're a Horse like the Western zodiac. No. Horse is just 1 of your 8 characters. The dominant character is your DAY Master.
See all 8 of your characters (free) → https://cheonmyeongdang.com/en/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d6

drafts.title: Four Pillars 102: Month
drafts.hook: Saju 102 — Pillar #2 of 4: the MONTH PILLAR. This is your career operating system. Why you naturally gravitate toward certain industries. Most overlooked pillar 🧵
body: Month Pillar = the lunar/solar month you were born + its stem-branch pair. It governs the 'palace' of career, mid-life, and how peers perceive you.
Born in summer months (May–July)? Strong fire energy. Tendency toward sales, performance, leadership, hospitality.
Born in winter months (Nov–Jan)? Strong water energy. Tendency toward analysis, research, finance, wisdom-based work.
Born in transitional months (Apr/Jul/Oct/Jan endings)? Earth energy = stability, real estate, food, mediation.
This is why career coaches who understand Saju can be eerily accurate — they're reading your Month Pillar before you've named your strengths.

drafts.title: Four Pillars 103: Day (the YOU pillar)
drafts.hook: Saju 103 — Pillar #3 of 4: the DAY PILLAR. This is literally YOU. Your Day Master (the Heavenly Stem of your Day Pillar) is the #1 most important character in your chart. Here's why 🧵
body: There are 10 possible Day Masters: Yang Wood, Yin Wood, Yang Fire, Yin Fire, Yang Earth, Yin Earth, Yang Metal, Yin Metal, Yang Water, Yin Water.
Your Day Master is the 'I' in your chart. Every other character is read in RELATION to it.

• Yang Wood = pine tree, ambitious, growth-driven
• Yin Fire = candle, intimate, intuitive
• Yang Metal = sword, decisive, justice-oriented
• Yin Water = mist, adaptive, deeply intuitive
Compatibility, career fit, dating type, even health tendencies — all start from the Day Master.
If a 'Saju reading' didn't tell you your Day Master, you didn't get a Saju reading. You got a horoscope.

drafts.title: Four Pillars 104: Hour
drafts.hook: Saju 104 — Pillar #4 of 4: the HOUR PILLAR. This is the most asked-about and least understood pillar. It governs your hidden self, late life, and children. Here's why your birth time matters 🧵
body: The day is divided into 12 two-hour blocks (시지). Each block has a unique stem-branch pair.
Born 23:00–00:59 = Rat hour. Born 11:00–12:59 = Horse hour. Etc.

What the Hour Pillar reveals:
• Hidden personality (what you don't show publicly)
• Children's karma
• Late-life tendencies (60+)
• Dream/inner-life patterns
If you don't know your birth time, Saju still works on 6 of 8 characters — but you lose the 'inner self' read. That's why Korean grandparents wrote birth times on family registries for centuries.
Don't know your time? Most countries have it on the long-form birth certificate.

drafts.title: 12 Unseong: your life-stage map
drafts.hook: Saju has a concept Western astrology completely lacks: 12 운성 (Unseong) — the life-stage of every character in your chart. From 'fetus' to 'tomb,' it tells you where each part of your life is in its cycle 🧵
body: The 12 stages: 장생 (birth), 목욕 (bath), 관대 (coming-of-age), 건록 (peak), 제왕 (king), 쇠 (decline), 병 (sickness), 사 (death), 묘 (tomb), 절 (cut-off), 태 (fetus), 양 (gestation).
Each character in your 8 has a stage. Example: Day Master 고 (Yang Wood) born in summer = 제왕 stage = peak strength.
Same Day Master born in autumn = 쇠 stage = declining strength = needs support.
Why it matters: it tells you when to push (peak stages) and when to recover (death/tomb stages). Career timing without this is guessing.

body:
10 Gods, paired by polarity:
• 비견/곁재 = peers/competitors

• 편재/정재 = wealth (variable/stable)
• 편관/정관 = authority (challenge/legitimate)
• 편인/정인 = knowledge/protection
Your 8 characters generate a unique mix of these 10. Strong Wealth gods? Money-magnet (also money-spender).
Strong Authority gods? Natural leader (also pressure-prone).
Strong Knowledge gods? Academic/researcher (also overthinker).

drafts.title: Why 1966 was bad for one country only
drafts.hook: Last Red Horse year (1966) hit Japan uniquely hard — birth rate dropped 25% from a single superstition. Here's the story, and what 2026 means for the modern version of that fear 🧵
body: Hinoeuma superstition (Japan, since Edo period): women born in Bing-Wu (Red Horse) years are 'too strong-willed' and unsuitable as wives.
1966: parents collectively delayed/avoided births. Annual births fell from ~1.82M to ~1.36M. Single largest fertility shock in modern Japan.
Korea historically had a similar superstition but it was less binding, and it has fully faded. China never had it.
2026: Japan's birth rate is already at crisis lows. Demographers are watching whether even modern, secular Japanese parents quietly avoid Red Horse births again.
The data point itself doesn't matter. What matters: superstitions move populations. Saju is the systematic version of that intuition — surfaced and analyzed.
Read your own chart instead of inheriting a superstition (free) → https://cheonmyeongdang.com/en/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d20

drafts.title: K-drama character archetypes via Sipsin

Why does every K-drama have the same 5 character types? Korean writers know Saju. Here are the 5 Ten Gods archetypes — and the K-drama trope each one became 🧵
(Note: trope analysis, not real-person reads.)
body: 1. PEER GODS (비견곁재) → the rival/best friend who pushes the lead. The chaebol heir's loyal cousin. The idol's competitive bandmate.
2. OUTPUT GODS (식신상관) → the artist/free spirit. The ramyeon shop owner with a past. The webtoon-drawing love interest.
3. WEALTH GODS (재성) → the ambitious lead. CEO romance. The flower-shop heiress. Money-as-character.
4. AUTHORITY GODS (관성) → the prosecutor, surgeon, detective. The 'stoic-with-a-secret' male lead.
5. KNOWLEDGE GODS (인성) → the wise grandma, the protective senior, the encyclopedic side character.

body:

動かない方が良い:
• 3月(金・年と衰突)
• 10月(土重複・停滞パターン)
これは「社会全体の潮」。あなた個人の命式で調整が入ります。
個人的な2026年月別タイミングを見る(無料)→ https://cheonmyeongdang.com/ja/?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=v2_d29
drafts.cta: 月別タイミングを見る → cheonmyeongdang.com/ja
